channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Quote of the day by Netscape co-founder Marc Andreessen: 'Software is eating the world' — a pithy assessment of the modern tech landscape
item.description: The venture capitalist and tech entrepreneur explained in a famous essay how software infiltrated every industry

item.title: Lanterns trailer: Hal Jordan and John Stewart team up for a Manhunter murder mystery
item.description: "Lanterns," starring Kyle Chandler and Aaron Pierre, premieres Aug. 16 on HBO and HBO Max.

item.title: Oracle awarded 10-year $7 billion Pentagon deal to supply Department of War with vital software and services
item.description: The contract unifies existing Oracle licensing under a single vehicle, and is expected to save $441 million across the 10-year period.

item.title: Vietnam is looking to restrict social media for kids; here are the growing number of other countries doing the same
item.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

item.title: Queen of the Damned: Everything we know about Season 4 of Interview with the Vampire
item.description: The Vampire Lestat is coming back for "Queen of the Damned," Season 4 of AMC's popular adaptation of Anne Rice's Vampire Chronicles series.

item.title: Paramount agrees to delay Warner Bros. merger into 2027
item.description: The extended pause will give time to hear cases for blocking the deal from states and the WGA.

item.title: Waymo reportedly mulling a breakup with Uber
item.description: The contract between the two companies ends in May 2028, Uber told TechCrunch.

item.title: Chinese Companies Are Selling Vapes With Chemicals Potentially More Potent Than Nicotine
item.description: Big Tobacco studied nicotine analogs like 6-methyl-nicotine for decades but never marketed them. Now, Chinese vape makers are using them to sidestep US regulations.

item.title: Europe’s central bank gets hilarious alternative designs for the euro banknote after asking for feedback
item.description: From water bottle caps to random people’s pets, social media has some interesting ideas for what Europe should put on its money. Asking the internet for advice can be a dicey game: Users can either be helpful or take the chance to make a joke of it all—as the European Central Bank (ECB) has come to find out.
